title: potentilla micrantha var. carniolica
intro: Potentilla micrantha var. carniolica, also known as Alpine Cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects. Its health benefits include the potential to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Alpine Cinquefoil typically involve drying the plant parts and using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
